req.title: Create observation (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a clinician can POST an Observation with a LOINC-coded 'code' element (positive path), that omitting 'code' yields HTTP 422 (negative path), and boundary conditions such as a code object present but with an empty 'coding' array must also be exercised. Additional negative paths cover missing subject reference and insufficient role (non-clinician). State transition coverage tests that an Observation in 'final' status cannot be re-registered. Together these tests achieve EP (valid/invalid partitions), BVA (minimal vs absent coding array), NEG (permission denial, missing fields), STC (status machine), and DT (combinatorial mandatory-field matrix).

tests.name: test_create_observation_valid_loinc_code_returns_201
preconditions: Patient with id <PATIENT_ID> exists in the system
actions: Build a well-formed FHIR Observation payload with resourceType, status, subject, and a LOINC code element
POST the payload to /Observation with clinician credentials
Capture the HTTP response status and body
trace: R-HLT-055: clinician creates Observation with LOINC code -> stored

tests.name: test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422
preconditions: Patient with id <PATIENT_ID> exists in the system
actions: Build an Observation payload that omits the mandatory 'code' element entirely
POST the payload to /Observation with clinician credentials
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error descriptor
Error message references 'code' as the missing element
trace: R-HLT-055: Observations without code shall be rejected -> 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422(clinician_auth_headers, existing_patient_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "preliminary",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    raw = str(body).lower()
    assert "code" in raw

tests.name: test_create_observation_empty_coding_array_returns_422
preconditions: Patient with id <PATIENT_ID> exists in the system
actions: Build an Observation payload where 'code' is present but 'coding' is an empty array (length 0 — boundary between absent and present)
POST the payload to /Observation with clinician credentials
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: System rejects a code element with zero coding entries as insufficient
trace: R-HLT-055: LOINC-coded code element required; empty coding array is boundary at zero entries

tests.name: test_create_observation_non_clinician_role_returns_403
preconditions: Patient with id <PATIENT_ID> exists in the system
actions: Build a fully valid FHIR Observation payload with LOINC code
POST the payload to /Observation using non-clinician credentials
expected: Observation is NOT created in the system
trace: R-HLT-055: permission denial negative path; actor must be role:clinician

tests.name: test_create_observation_missing_subject_returns_422
actions: Build an Observation payload with valid LOINC code but no 'subject' reference
POST the payload to /Observation with clinician credentials
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: Error body references 'subject' as missing mandatory element
trace: R-HLT-055: missing mandatory element (subject) negative path; DT combination: code=present, subject=absent

tests.name: test_re_register_final_observation_returns_conflict
preconditions: Patient with id <PATIENT_ID> exists in the system
An Observation with status 'final' already exists with id <FINAL_OBS_ID>
actions: Attempt to POST a new Observation with the same logical identifier referencing or duplicating the existing 'final' Observation
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict or HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity
System rejects the conflicting state transition from 'final' back to 'preliminary' or duplicate registration
trace: R-HLT-055: conflicting state transitions negative path; 'final' observations must not be re-registered
